description: Open-source, mobile-first AI music sketchpad. Generate music with MiniMax, stream final audio chunks, stack blocks in a vertical arrangement, perform non-destructive edits, branch variations via take DAG, and export rough mixes.

userStories.title: Scaffold SvelteKit 2 project with Svelte 5, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn-svelte, and Cloudflare adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the base project scaffolded with pinned dependencies so that all subsequent stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: SvelteKit 2 project created with Svelte 5 in the repo root
Tailwind CSS v4 installed and configured
shadcn-svelte (v2+) installed and initialized
Cloudflare Pages adapter configured in svelte.config.js
wrangler.jsonc created with basic Pages configuration
All dependency versions pinned to exact minor versions in package.json
Dev server starts successfully with `npm run dev`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. Every other story depends on this.

userStories.title: Install and configure Drizzle ORM with Postgres connection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Drizzle ORM connected to Postgres so that database access is available for schema and queries.
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle-orm and drizzle-kit installed
postgres (pg) driver installed for local dev; @neondatabase/serverless installed for production
drizzle.config.ts reads DATABASE_URL from env
Database client module created at /src/lib/server/db.ts (or similar) exporting a typed Drizzle instance
.env created with DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/grande_studio for local development
.env.example includes DATABASE_URL placeholder
Local Postgres database 'grande_studio' created if it does not exist (via createdb or equivalent)
Connection works with `npx drizzle-kit push` or `npx drizzle-kit generate` (no errors against local Postgres)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Local dev uses local Postgres. Neon is the production target only.

userStories.title: Migration-zero: projects and audio_assets tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the projects and audio_assets tables created so that the core data model exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Drizzle schema file defines projects table: id (uuid PK), owner_id (uuid not null), title (text not null), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at

Drizzle schema file defines audio_assets table: id (uuid PK), project_id (FK to projects), owner_id, source_type ('generated'|'uploaded'|'imported'|'rendered_export'), provider ('minimax'|'elevenlabs'|'stability'|'local_upload'|'browser_render'), provider_model (nullable), provider_job_id (nullable), title, prompt (nullable), lyrics (nullable), structure_tags_json (jsonb nullable), input_asset_ids_json (jsonb nullable), r2_object_key (nullable), duration_sec (numeric nullable), format (nullable), sample_rate (int nullable), bit_depth (int nullable), status ('created'|'queued'|'generating'|'receiving_audio'|'persisting'|'ready'|'failed'|'deleted'), error_json (jsonb nullable), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
Indexes on audio_assets: owner_id, project_id
Migration generated and runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migration-zero: arrangement_clips and take_edges tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the arrangement and take relationship tables so that clips and variation branching can be stored.
acceptanceCriteria: arrangement_clips table: id (uuid PK), project_id (FK), owner_id, asset_id (FK to audio_assets), start_time_sec (numeric default 0), trim_start_sec (numeric default 0), trim_end_sec (numeric nullable), clip_duration_sec (numeric not null), gain_db (numeric default 0), muted (boolean default false), soloed (boolean default false), layer_order (int default 0), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at

take_edges table: id (uuid PK), project_id (FK), owner_id, parent_asset_id (FK), child_asset_id (FK), branch_type ('prompt_variation'|'lyric_edit'|'instrumental_variant'|'cover_restyle'|'reference_from_asset'|'manual_duplicate'|'other'), branch_prompt (nullable), created_at, deleted_at
take_edges has CHECK constraint: parent_asset_id <> child_asset_id
Indexes on arrangement_clips: owner_id, project_id, asset_id
Indexes on take_edges: owner_id, project_id, parent_asset_id, child_asset_id
Migration generated and runs successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No loop_enabled boolean — looping is implicit when clip_duration_sec > trimmed audio length. No pan column — deferred to post-V1.

userStories.title: Migration-zero: generation_jobs, quota_reservations, export_jobs, and provider_events tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the job tracking, quota, export, and provider event tables so that generation lifecycle is fully modeled.
acceptanceCriteria: generation_jobs table: id (uuid PK), project_id (FK), owner_id, provider, provider_model, job_type ('text_to_music'|'instrumental'|'cover_restyle'), status ('created'|'queued'|'generating'|'receiving_audio'|'persisting'|'succeeded'|'failed'|'cancelled'), request_json (jsonb not null), response_json (jsonb nullable), error_json (jsonb nullable), error_code (text nullable), quota_reservation_id (uuid nullable), resulting_asset_id (FK nullable), idempotency_key (text not null), created_at, updated_at

quota_reservations table: id (uuid PK), owner_id, generation_job_id (FK nullable), idempotency_key (text not null), units_reserved (int not null), status ('reserved'|'committed'|'released'|'expired'), expires_at (timestamptz not null), created_at, updated_at

export_jobs table: id (uuid PK), project_id (FK), owner_id, export_type ('block_source'|'rough_mixdown'), status ('created'|'rendering_client'|'uploading'|'ready'|'failed'), snapshot_version (int default 1), arrangement_snapshot_json (jsonb nullable), r2_object_key (nullable), error_json (jsonb nullable), created_at, updated_at
provider_events table: id (uuid PK), owner_id, generation_job_id (FK nullable), provider, event_type, payload_json (jsonb nullable), created_at
Indexes on generation_jobs: owner_id, project_id, resulting_asset_id, idempotency_key
Migration generated and runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enable RLS and create ownership policies for all user-owned tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Postgres RLS enforcing owner isolation so that users can never access other users' data.
acceptanceCriteria: RLS is enabled on: projects, audio_assets, arrangement_clips, take_edges, generation_jobs, quota_reservations, export_jobs, provider_events
Each table has a SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy referencing current_setting('app.user_id', true)
A helper function or SQL snippet sets SET LOCAL app.user_id before every data access in SvelteKit endpoints
Migrations for RLS policies run successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Application-layer checks are required in addition to RLS, but RLS is the enforcement backstop.

userStories.title: Install and configure BetterAuth with email/password provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need authentication infrastructure so that users can create accounts and maintain sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: better-auth package installed
Auth server configured at /src/lib/server/auth.ts with email/password provider
BetterAuth Drizzle adapter configured to use the local Postgres database
Auth API route handler created (e.g., /src/routes/api/auth/[...all]/+server.ts)
Auth client created at /src/lib/auth-client.ts
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET auto-generated and added to .env (e.g., via openssl rand -base64 32)
BETTER_AUTH_URL set to http://localhost:5173 in .env
Both vars added to .env.example as placeholders
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Agent should auto-generate the secret and set the local URL. User does not need to provide these.

userStories.title: Create sign-up and sign-in pages

Sign-in page at /signin with email and password fields and submit button
Form validation: email format, password minimum length
Error messages displayed for invalid credentials or existing email
Successful sign-up redirects to workspace
Successful sign-in redirects to workspace
Pages use shadcn-svelte form components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add auth session middleware, sign-out, and protected route layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want my session to persist and I want to sign out when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: SvelteKit hooks.server.ts validates session on each request
Session user is available in page data via layout server load function
Sign-out button in workspace header ends session and redirects to sign-in
Unauthenticated access to protected routes redirects to sign-in (but workspace root allows unauthenticated access for temp project)
Session persists across page reloads (cookie-based)

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create provider registry types and provider interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed provider abstractions so that MiniMax (and future providers) are behind a clean adapter boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: ProviderId type: 'minimax' | 'elevenlabs' | 'stability'
ProviderRegistryEntry interface with id, enabled, stub, displayName
V1 registry: minimax enabled, elevenlabs stub/disabled, stability stub/disabled
MusicProvider interface with: generateTextToMusic, generateInstrumental, generateCoverRestyle, optional streamGenerationAudio, optional preprocessCoverSource
Input/output types defined: TextToMusicInput, InstrumentalGenerationInput, CoverRestyleInput, ProviderGenerationHandle, ProviderAudioChunk
All types at /src/lib/providers/types.ts
Registry at /src/lib/providers/registry.ts
No 'any' in provider types
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create MiniMax request validator with full test matrix
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized MiniMax validation module so that no endpoint or UI duplicates validation rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Validator module at /src/lib/providers/minimax/validateMusicRequest.ts
Validates: prompt non-empty
Validates: instrumental true + lyrics = invalid (clear error, not silent ignore)
Validates: non-instrumental + no lyrics + no optimizer = invalid
Validates: non-instrumental + lyrics = valid
Validates: non-instrumental + no lyrics + optimizer on = valid
Validates: unsupported structure tags produce warning
Validates: cover/re-style requires source asset
Validates: cover/re-style without source = invalid
Validates: lyrics length within MiniMax limits
Test file at /src/lib/providers/minimax/validateMusicRequest.test.ts with all above cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the single source of truth for MiniMax validation. No ad-hoc validation elsewhere.

userStories.title: Create MiniMax adapter: text-to-music, instrumental, and lyrics request formatting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MiniMax adapter to format generation requests correctly so that the workflow can call MiniMax through a typed boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: MiniMax adapter at /src/lib/providers/minimax/adapter.ts implements MusicProvider interface
generateTextToMusic formats prompt payload for MiniMax Music 2.6 API
generateInstrumental sets is_instrumental=true in payload
Lyrics are included in payload when provided and instrumental is false
Lyrics optimizer/autogeneration flag is passed through when supported
API key read from environment variable MINIMAX_API_KEY (never exposed client-side)
No 'any' types in request/response handling
Mocked tests verify correct payload formatting for each generation mode
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create MiniMax adapter: cover/re-style payload mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the adapter to handle cover/re-style requests so that users can generate variations from existing audio.
acceptanceCriteria: generateCoverRestyle method in MiniMax adapter accepts source audio URL/buffer and optional lyrics
Payload formatted for MiniMax music-cover endpoint
Source audio reference uses R2-stored file (not a provider URL)
Lyrics are optional in cover/re-style mode
Mocked test verifies cover/re-style payload structure
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create MiniMax adapter: streaming hex response handler and error normalization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the adapter to handle MiniMax's streaming hex output and normalize errors so that the workflow receives clean typed data.
acceptanceCriteria: streamGenerationAudio method yields ProviderAudioChunk objects from MiniMax hex-encoded stream
Each chunk contains decoded audio bytes
Stream termination is detected correctly
Non-streaming URL fallback: if URL mode is used, adapter returns the URL (workflow must fetch immediately)
Provider errors are caught and normalized to typed error objects with error codes: provider_timeout, provider_validation_error, provider_auth_error, provider_rate_limited
Provider responses are normalized before leaving adapter code
Mocked test: streaming hex chunks assembled correctly
Mocked test: error normalization for each error type
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MiniMax streaming hex chunks are the final output bytes delivered incrementally, not a separate preview artifact.

userStories.title: Create asset and job status state machine services with tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need state machine services that enforce valid status transitions so that assets and jobs never reach invalid states.
acceptanceCriteria: Asset status state machine at /src/lib/services/asset-status.ts

Allowed asset transitions: created->queued, queued->generating, queued->failed, generating->receiving_audio, generating->failed, receiving_audio->persisting, receiving_audio->failed, persisting->ready, persisting->failed, ready->deleted, failed->deleted
Disallowed: failed->queued, failed->generating, ready->failed, ready->receiving_audio
Job status state machine at /src/lib/services/job-status.ts

Allowed job transitions: created->queued, queued->generating, queued->cancelled, generating->receiving_audio, generating->failed, receiving_audio->persisting, receiving_audio->failed, persisting->succeeded, persisting->failed
Disallowed: failed->queued, failed->generating, succeeded->failed, cancelled->generating
Both services export a validateTransition(current, next) function that returns valid/invalid with reason
Test file covers all allowed and disallowed transitions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Failed jobs are immutable. Retry always creates a new job.

userStories.title: Create quota reservation service with tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need quota reservation logic so that generations are metered and double-generation is prevented.
acceptanceCriteria: Quota service at /src/lib/services/quota.ts
reserveQuota(ownerId, jobId, idempotencyKey): creates reservation with 10-minute TTL and 'reserved' status
commitQuota(reservationId): transitions to 'committed'
releaseQuota(reservationId): transitions to 'released'
expireStaleReservations(): transitions expired 'reserved' rows to 'expired'
Allowed transitions: reserved->committed, reserved->released, reserved->expired
Disallowed: committed->released, expired->committed, released->committed
Idempotency key prevents duplicate reservations on repeated calls
checkDailyUsage(ownerId): returns count of committed reservations today
Test file covers all transitions, idempotency, and daily usage check
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Daily limit is 10 for authenticated users. Resets at midnight UTC.

userStories.title: Create take edge service with cycle prevention and tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a take edge service so that asset derivation relationships are stored and cycles are prevented.
acceptanceCriteria: Take edge service at /src/lib/services/take-edges.ts
createEdge(projectId, ownerId, parentAssetId, childAssetId, branchType, branchPrompt?): creates edge row
Before creating edge, service checks for cycles (BFS/DFS from child to see if parent is reachable)
If cycle detected, returns error without creating edge
getChildren(assetId): returns child edges
getParents(assetId): returns parent edges
Deleting an asset soft-deletes dependent edges
Test file: create edge, get children, get parents, cycle detection (A->B->C->A rejected), self-edge rejected
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create R2 storage service for audio files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an R2 storage abstraction so that audio files can be uploaded, downloaded, and served with signed URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: R2 service at /src/lib/services/r2-storage.ts
uploadAudio(objectKey, bytes, contentType): uploads to R2 bucket
getSignedUrl(objectKey, expiresIn?): returns time-limited signed URL for playback
deleteObject(objectKey): deletes from R2
Object key convention: {ownerId}/{projectId}/{assetId}.{ext}
R2 bucket binding configured in wrangler.jsonc
R2_BUCKET_NAME env var in .env.example
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Provider URLs must never be stored as canonical audio source. Only R2 object keys are canonical.

userStories.title: Create file upload endpoint for cover/re-style source audio
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server endpoint that validates and persists uploaded audio files so that cover/re-style has source material.
acceptanceCriteria: POST endpoint at /api/upload (or SvelteKit form action)
Validates session (rejects unauthenticated)
Validates file size: max 50MB
Validates file type: MP3, WAV, M4A, FLAC only
Persists file to R2 via R2 storage service
Creates audio_asset row with source_type='uploaded', provider='local_upload', status='ready'
Sets r2_object_key on the asset
Returns asset ID on success
Returns clear error messages for size/type violations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create generation endpoint: validate, create job + asset + quota reservation, enqueue
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the server endpoint that initiates a generation so that the frontend can submit prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: POST endpoint at /api/generate (or SvelteKit form action)
Validates session and sets DB user context for RLS
Validates request through MiniMax validator (/src/lib/providers/minimax/validateMusicRequest.ts)
Checks daily quota via quota service; rejects with clear message if limit reached
Creates audio_asset row with status='created'
Creates generation_job row with status='created' and idempotency_key
Creates quota_reservation with status='reserved' and 10-minute TTL
Enqueues job to Cloudflare Queue
Returns job ID and asset ID to client
If validation fails, no job/asset/reservation is created
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Idempotency key prevents double-generation from repeated clicks.

userStories.title: Create Cloudflare Workflow scaffold: consume queue, update statuses
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Cloudflare Workflow that processes generation jobs so that MiniMax calls happen durably in the background.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloudflare Queue configured in wrangler.jsonc
Cloudflare Workflow consumer defined that receives job messages
Workflow reads generation_job and audio_asset from DB
Workflow updates job status to 'queued' then 'generating'
Workflow updates asset status in sync with job status
Workflow structure has clear steps for: provider call, audio assembly, R2 persist, finalize
If job not found, workflow logs error and exits
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Workflow: call MiniMax adapter, receive streaming hex, assemble bytes, persist to R2
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core Workflow execution path so that generation produces a persisted audio file.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow calls MiniMax adapter with stream=true via streamGenerationAudio
Workflow updates asset status to 'receiving_audio' when chunks begin
Workflow assembles hex-decoded chunks into final audio byte array
Workflow updates asset status to 'persisting' before R2 write
Workflow writes final audio file to R2 via R2 storage service
Workflow updates asset: status='ready', r2_object_key set, duration_sec/format/sample_rate populated where detectable
Workflow updates job: status='succeeded'
Non-streaming fallback: if URL mode used, workflow fetches URL immediately and streams to R2 (URL never stored)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the happy path. Error handling is in the next story.

userStories.title: Implement Workflow: error handling, status transitions, and quota commit/release
userStories.description: As a developer, I need robust error handling in the Workflow so that failures are tracked and quota is managed correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: If provider returns validation error: job and asset to 'failed', error_code='provider_validation_error', quota released
If provider times out: job and asset to 'failed', error_code='provider_timeout', quota released
If provider auth fails: error_code='provider_auth_error', quota released
If provider rate limits: error_code='provider_rate_limited', quota released
If stream breaks before valid file: error_code='stream_interrupted', job and asset to 'failed'
If audio assembly fails: error_code='audio_assembly_failed'
If R2 write fails: error_code='r2_write_failed', job and asset to 'failed'
On success: quota reservation committed
On failure before provider consumption: quota reservation released
Failed jobs are never mutated back to a non-failed state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create SSE endpoint for job status updates with session validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server-sent events endpoint so that the client receives real-time generation status changes.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE endpoint at /api/events (or /api/sse)
Validates session; rejects unauthenticated (except temp sessions)
Streams job status change events for the authenticated user's jobs
Event format includes: jobId, assetId, status, errorCode (if failed)
Connection stays open until client disconnects
Handles client disconnect gracefully (no resource leaks)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire client to consume SSE and update block status reactively
userStories.description: As a creator, I want block cards to update in real-time when my generation progresses so that I see status changes without refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Client-side SSE consumer module at /src/lib/stores/sse.svelte.ts (or similar)
Connects to SSE endpoint on workspace load
Parses incoming status events and updates relevant asset/job state in stores
Block cards re-render when their asset status changes
Automatic reconnection on connection drop with exponential backoff
Connection closed on navigation away from workspace
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Forward audio chunks from Workflow through SSE for live-listening
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to hear audio as it generates so that I don't wait in silence.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow optionally forwards decoded audio chunks to a live-listening channel (e.g., via Cloudflare pub/sub, KV, or direct SSE push)
SSE endpoint forwards audio chunk events to the client for the relevant job
Client audio engine can receive and play incoming chunks as they arrive
Block card shows 'Live' indicator while receiving audio chunks
If live-listening fails but R2 persistence succeeds, asset still becomes ready
Live stream is not labeled as a separate preview artifact
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a best-effort feature. Generation success does not depend on live-listening working.

userStories.title: Create audio engine module with AudioContext lifecycle and buffer loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a headless audio engine so that all audio behavior is isolated from Svelte components.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio engine module at /src/lib/audio-engine/engine.ts
Implements AudioEngine interface with loadAsset, unloadAsset, dispose methods
Creates AudioContext on first user interaction (respects browser autoplay policy)
Manages AudioContext lifecycle: create, suspend, resume, close
loadAsset(assetId, url): fetches audio from URL, decodes to AudioBuffer, stores by assetId
unloadAsset(assetId): removes stored buffer
dispose(): closes AudioContext and clears all buffers
Can be tested with mocked AudioContext
Svelte components never directly instantiate AudioContext
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement single-asset playback with transport controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to play/pause/stop a single audio asset so that block preview playback works.
acceptanceCriteria: play(): starts playback from current position using AudioBufferSourceNode
pause(): suspends AudioContext, preserving position
stop(): stops playback, resets position to 0
seek(timeSec): sets playback position
Engine exposes reactive state: currentTime (number), isPlaying (boolean)
currentTime updates during playback (via requestAnimationFrame or AudioContext.currentTime)
Can play a single loaded asset by ID
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement per-clip gain, mute, and solo in audio engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to support per-clip mixing so that stacked playback has volume and solo control.
acceptanceCriteria: setClipGain(clipId, gainDb): sets GainNode value for the clip
setClipMute(clipId, muted): disconnects/reconnects clip audio path
setClipSolo(clipId, soloed): marks clip as soloed
Solo semantics: if no clips soloed, all non-muted clips audible
Solo semantics: if any clips soloed, only soloed clips audible
Solo semantics: multiple soloed clips play simultaneously
Solo semantics: mute state persists while solo active; clearing all solos restores prior mute states
Each clip has its own GainNode in the audio graph
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement clip start offset scheduling and trim region playback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to schedule clips at offsets and respect trim boundaries so that arrangement timing works.
acceptanceCriteria: setClipStartOffset(clipId, startTimeSec): clip begins at this time on the transport timeline
setClipTrim(clipId, trimStartSec, trimEndSec): only the trimmed region of the source buffer plays
During play(), clips scheduled to start at their offset relative to transport time
Clips that start after current transport position are scheduled for the future
Clips whose start+duration is before current transport position are skipped
Trim and offset work together: clip plays trimmed region starting at offset time
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement drag-to-extend looping in audio engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to loop a clip's trimmed region within clip_duration_sec so that extended clips repeat seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: setClipLoop(clipId, clipDurationSec): sets the total playback duration for the clip
If clipDurationSec > trimmed audio length, the trimmed region repeats
Partial loops supported: if clipDurationSec = 2.5 * trimmed length, audio plays 2 full loops + 0.5 of a third
If clipDurationSec <= trimmed audio length, no looping occurs (clip plays once, possibly shorter)
Looping implemented by scheduling multiple AudioBufferSourceNodes or using loop property with correct boundaries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: V1 does not guarantee seamless beat-perfect loops. DAW-standard drag-to-extend behavior.

userStories.title: Implement synchronized multi-clip playback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to play multiple clips simultaneously from a shared clock so that arrangement stacking works.
acceptanceCriteria: setArrangement(clips: ArrangementClipState[]): configures all clips for playback
play() schedules all non-muted, non-solo-excluded clips on the same AudioContext clock
Each clip uses its own start offset, trim, gain, mute/solo, and clip_duration_sec
Arrangement duration = max(clip.startTimeSec + clip.clipDurationSec) across all clips
Transport stops when arrangement end is reached
Adding/removing clips while stopped correctly updates the arrangement
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create arrangement store as live source of truth
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Svelte 5 runes store for arrangement state so that UI and audio engine share a reactive data source.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrangement store at /src/lib/stores/arrangement.svelte.ts
Uses Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived) for reactivity
Stores array of ArrangementClipState objects
Exposes methods: addClip, removeClip, updateClip, reorderClips
Exposes derived: totalDuration, activeClips (non-muted/solo-aware), clipCount
Store is the single source of truth; audio engine and UI consume it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire audio engine to consume arrangement store reactively
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audio engine to react to arrangement store changes so that playback reflects the current arrangement.
acceptanceCriteria: When arrangement store clips change, audio engine's setArrangement is called
When a clip's gain/mute/solo changes in the store, the corresponding engine method is called
When a clip's trim/offset/duration changes in the store, the engine updates scheduling
Reactive binding uses Svelte 5 $effect or equivalent
Engine updates do not cause audible glitches when not playing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement debounced DB persistence and hydration for arrangement state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need arrangement state to persist to DB and reload on project open so that edits survive browser close.
acceptanceCriteria: Clip edits (gain, mute, solo, trim, offset, duration, layer order) trigger debounced DB writes (500-1000ms debounce)
Destructive actions (remove clip) persist immediately with no debounce
On project load, arrangement store is hydrated from arrangement_clips DB rows
Hydration populates all clip fields correctly including clip_duration_sec
DB writes use the RLS-protected connection (SET LOCAL app.user_id)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create main workspace layout with project header
userStories.description: As a creator, I want a clean workspace layout so that I can access all features from one screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Main workspace page at / (root route) or /workspace
Layout includes regions: project header, generate panel area, asset list area, arrangement area with transport
Project header shows: project title (editable inline), remaining daily quota (e.g., '7/10'), sign-out button
Authenticated users see their project; unauthenticated users see a temp workspace
On first authenticated visit, auto-creates a default project with title 'My Project'
On subsequent visits, loads existing project
Layout is mobile-first and responsive
Uses shadcn-svelte components for UI elements
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create generate panel with prompt field and generate button
userStories.description: As a creator, I want a prompt input and generate button so that I can start creating music.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate panel component rendered in workspace layout
